hero.label: Made for HappyRobot
hero.headline: I want to build pipeline for AI that runs real operations.
hero.subheadline: HappyRobot is replacing manual phone calls in logistics with AI agents that actually work. I spent a year doing cold outreach at an AI startup, and now I want to bring that same grit to a team that's scaling voice AI across the real economy.

whyCompany.title: Voice AI for logistics is a massive unlock
whyCompany.body: Truck drivers, brokers, and carriers still run on phone calls. HappyRobot isn't just automating support tickets. You're replacing the most friction-heavy communication layer in supply chains. That's a $4T+ market running on hold music and voicemails.

whyCompany.title: $62M raised, a16z + YC backed, and hiring SDRs now
whyCompany.body: A $44M Series B means HappyRobot is past product-market fit and ready to scale GTM. For an SDR joining now, the timing is perfect: the product works, the market is huge, and the pipeline machine needs building. That's exactly the stage where I thrive.

whyCompany.title: I've done this before at an AI startup
whyCompany.body: At Copygen.ai, I built outbound pipeline from zero. Cold calls, LinkedIn sequences, HubSpot workflows. I helped land 15-20 paying B2B clients. Then at NextAfter, I learned how to think about digital fundraising strategy at a consultancy level. Both experiences map directly to selling AI to enterprise logistics teams.

quote: I've spent the last year convincing companies to trust AI with their workflows. HappyRobot is doing that at a scale that actually matters. I want in.
